title: SecurityFindingsReport
description: Canonical schema for all security audit findings. Claude must write to this schema when adding findings so reports remain machine-readable and diffable across runs.

report_type.description: The type of audit that produced this report.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the audit ran.

target.description: Directory path or URL that was audited.

id.description: Unique finding ID. Format: TOOL-YYYYMMDD-NNN (e.g. STATIC-20260101-001). Stable across re-runs for the same issue.

severity.description: CRITICAL=exploitable now with high impact. HIGH=significant risk. MEDIUM=notable but needs context. LOW=best practice gap. INFO=observation.

category.description: OWASP category. Use format: A01:BrokenAccessControl, API7:SSRF, LLM01:PromptInjection, etc.

rule.description: Short machine-readable rule name that triggered this finding.

title.example: SQL query built via string concatenation

description.description: What the issue is and why it's a risk. Be specific — reference the actual code or config.

file.description: Relative path to the affected file, if applicable.

line.description: Line number of the finding, if applicable.

match.description: The matched code snippet. Redact actual secret values — show location only.

remediation.description: Concrete fix. Include a code example where possible.

references.description: Links to OWASP, CWE, CVE, or other references.

false_positive_notes.description: Conditions under which this finding might be a false positive. Claude should populate this when reviewing.
